description: Fully migrate from ESLint + Prettier to Biome across the entire monorepo, all plugins, docs, scaffold templates, build scripts, CLAUDE.md files, and skill files. Remove all ESLint and Prettier dependencies, configs, and references. The codebase must be completely ESLint-free and Prettier-free after this work.

userStories.title: Create root biome.json configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a root biome.json that replaces the ESLint and Prettier configs so that all platform packages use Biome for linting and formatting.
acceptanceCriteria: A `biome.json` exists at the repo root with formatter settings matching the current .prettierrc (singleQuote, trailingComma all, semi, arrowParens avoid/asNeeded, printWidth 120, bracketSameLine true)

Linter rules are configured to match current ESLint rules: noDoubleEquals (eqeqeq), useConst (prefer-const), noVar, useConsistentArrowReturn (arrow-body-style), useImportType (consistent-type-imports), useExportType (consistent-type-exports), noUnusedVariables, noUnusedImports, noUnusedFunctionParameters
Import organization is configured with groups matching the current import-x/order config: URLs, node builtins, packages, @* internal aliases, relative paths
React and a11y rules are enabled via domains (react: recommended)
File ignores match current .prettierignore and eslint ignores (dist/, node_modules/, plugins/**, docs/**, storybook-static/, .ralph/worktrees/**, .claude/worktrees/**, .tmp/**)
The useSortedClasses nursery rule is enabled for Tailwind class sorting
JSON and markdown formatting is enabled
npm run build passes, npm run type-check passes, npm run test passes
userStories.notes: Create `biome.json` at the repo root. Key formatter settings from current `.prettierrc`: trailingComma='all' -> trailingCommas='all', semi=true -> semicolons='always', singleQuote=true -> quoteStyle='single', arrowParens='avoid' -> arrowParentheses='asNeeded', printWidth=120 -> lineWidth=120, bracketSameLine=true -> bracketSameLine=true. Use indentStyle='space' with indentWidth=2 (matching current Prettier default). For linting, enable recommended rules plus configure specific overrides. Use `linter.domains` for react and test domains. For import organizing, configure `assist.actions.source.organizeImports` with groups: [':URL:', ':NODE:', ':PACKAGE:', '@*/**', ':PATH:']. Enable `useSortedClasses` in nursery for Tailwind sorting. Do NOT delete any eslint/prettier files yet — just create the new biome.json. Install @biomejs/biome as a devDependency in the root package.json (use --save-exact per Biome's recommendation). The biome.json does NOT need to be covered by any tsconfig (it's JSON, not TS). Do NOT touch any other files besides biome.json and package.json (to add the dep).

userStories.title: Replace root package.json scripts and lint-staged with Biome commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want npm run lint, npm run format, and pre-commit hooks to use Biome so that the dev workflow uses the new tool.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run lint runs `biome lint .`
npm run lint:fix runs `biome lint --fix .`
npm run format runs `biome format --write .`
npm run format:check runs `biome check --formatter-enabled=true --linter-enabled=false .`
npm run check still runs build + lint + format:check + knip + test in sequence
lint-staged config in package.json is replaced: *.{ts,tsx} runs `biome check --write`, *.json runs `biome format --write`, *.md runs `biome format --write`
The .husky/pre-commit hook still works (it calls lint-staged which now invokes biome)
npm run build passes, npm run type-check passes, npm run lint passes, npm run knip passes, npm run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit `package.json` scripts section. Change: `lint` from `eslint .` to `biome lint .`, `lint:fix` from `eslint . --fix` to `biome lint --fix .`, `format` from `prettier --write --cache ...` to `biome format --write .`, `format:check` from `prettier --check ...` to `biome check --formatter-enabled=true --linter-enabled=false .` (or simply `biome format .` which is check-only by default). Update the `lint-staged` config object: for `*.{ts,tsx}` use `['biome check --write --no-errors-on-unmatched']`, for `*.json` use `['biome format --write --no-errors-on-unmatched']`, for `*.md` use `['biome format --write --no-errors-on-unmatched']`. The `--no-errors-on-unmatched` flag prevents errors when lint-staged passes files that biome doesn't recognize. Do NOT remove any devDependencies yet.

userStories.title: Delete root ESLint and Prettier config files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the old ESLint and Prettier config files removed so there's no ambiguity about which tool is in use.
acceptanceCriteria: eslint.config.ts is deleted from the repo root
.prettierrc is deleted from the repo root
.prettierignore is deleted from the repo root
tsconfig.configs.json no longer includes eslint.config.ts in its include array
scripts/check-tsconfig-coverage.ts no longer lists eslint.config.ts in ROOT_TS_FILES
npm run build passes, npm run type-check passes, npm run lint passes, npm run format:check passes, npm run knip passes, npm run test passes
userStories.notes: Delete three files: `eslint.config.ts`, `.prettierrc`, `.prettierignore`. Then update `tsconfig.configs.json` — remove `eslint.config.ts` from the `include` array (line 6). Update `scripts/check-tsconfig-coverage.ts` — remove `eslint.config.ts` from the `ROOT_TS_FILES` array (line 24, currently `['eslint.config.ts', 'knip.ts', 'playwright.config.ts']` — change to `['knip.ts', 'playwright.config.ts']`). After deletion, run `npm run build && npm run type-check` to verify nothing references the deleted files.

userStories.title: Remove all ESLint and Prettier devDependencies from root package.json
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all ESLint/Prettier npm packages removed from the root so the dependency tree is clean.
acceptanceCriteria: The following packages are removed from root devDependencies: @eslint/js, @types/eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y, @typescript-eslint/parser, eslint, eslint-config-prettier, eslint-import-resolver-typescript, eslint-plugin-import-x, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y, eslint-plugin-prettier, eslint-plugin-react, eslint-plugin-react-hooks, prettier, prettier-plugin-tailwindcss, typescript-eslint
The globals package is removed if no longer needed (check if biome.json handles browser/node globals)
knip.ts no longer has @typescript-eslint/parser in ignoreDependencies
npm install completes without errors
npm run build passes, npm run type-check passes, npm run lint passes, npm run knip passes, npm run test passes
userStories.notes: Remove these 14 packages from `devDependencies` in root `package.json`: `@eslint/js`, `@types/eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y`, `@typescript-eslint/parser`, `eslint`, `eslint-config-prettier`, `eslint-import-resolver-typescript`, `eslint-plugin-import-x`, `eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y`, `eslint-plugin-prettier`, `eslint-plugin-react`, `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`, `prettier`, `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss`, `typescript-eslint`. Check if `globals` (line 73) is still needed — it was only used by the ESLint config for browser/node globals; Biome handles this via domains and the `javascript.globals` config. If not needed elsewhere, remove it too. Also update `knip.ts` (line 9): remove `'@typescript-eslint/parser'` from the `ignoreDependencies` array in the root workspace config. After removing deps, run `npm install` to update the lockfile, then verify all checks pass.

userStories.title: Replace prettier usage in generate-browser-tools-catalog.ts with Biome
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the code generation script to use Biome for formatting generated TypeScript instead of Prettier.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/generate-browser-tools-catalog.ts uses biome format instead of prettier for formatting generated code
The generated output file is correctly formatted
npm run build passes (which runs generate:browser-tools-catalog), npm run type-check passes, npm run lint passes, npm run test passes
userStories.notes: In `scripts/generate-browser-tools-catalog.ts` (lines 80-85), the script currently runs `prettier --parser typescript` via `execFileSync` to format generated TypeScript. Replace with Biome: change the command from `join(repoRoot, 'node_modules', '.bin', 'prettier')` with args `['--parser', 'typescript']` to `join(repoRoot, 'node_modules', '.bin', 'biome')` with args `['format', '--stdin-file-path=generated.ts']`. Biome's `format` command reads from stdin when `--stdin-file-path` is specified (it uses the file extension to determine the parser). The `input` option in execFileSync already passes content via stdin, so the plumbing should work the same way. Verify by running `npm run generate:browser-tools-catalog` and checking the output file is still well-formatted.

userStories.title: Update scaffold template in platform/cli to generate Biome config instead of ESLint/Prettier
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want `opentabs plugin create` to scaffold a biome.json instead of eslint.config.ts and .prettierrc so new plugins use Biome from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: The scaffolded package.json has @biomejs/biome in devDependencies instead of eslint, eslint-config-prettier, eslint-plugin-prettier, prettier, typescript-eslint
The scaffolded package.json lint script runs `biome lint src/`
The scaffolded package.json lint:fix script runs `biome lint --fix src/`
The scaffolded package.json format:check script runs `biome format src/`
The scaffolded package.json format script runs `biome format --write src/`
A biome.json is scaffolded instead of eslint.config.ts and .prettierrc
The ESLINT_CONFIG_CONTENT and PRETTIERRC_CONTENT constants are removed and replaced with BIOME_CONFIG_CONTENT
The scaffolded README references biome instead of eslint
platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts tests are updated to verify biome.json is created (not eslint.config.ts/.prettierrc)
npm run build passes, npm run type-check passes, npm run lint passes, npm run knip passes, npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Edit `platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts`. Changes needed:

1. In `generatePackageJson` (lines 103-131): Replace scripts — `lint: 'biome lint src/'`, `lint:fix: 'biome lint --fix src/'`, `format:check: 'biome format src/'`, `format: 'biome format --write src/'`. The `check` script (line 111) should remain the same structure. Replace devDependencies — remove `eslint`, `eslint-config-prettier`, `eslint-plugin-prettier`, `prettier`, `typescript-eslint`; add `'@biomejs/biome': '<version>'` (use the same version installed at root — check root package.json for exact version).

2. Replace `ESLINT_CONFIG_CONTENT` (lines 162-189) and `PRETTIERRC_CONTENT` (lines 191-204) with a single `BIOME_CONFIG_CONTENT` constant that generates a biome.json matching the plugin convention: formatter settings (singleQuote, trailingCommas all, semicolons always, arrowParentheses asNeeded, lineWidth 120, bracketSameLine true, indentStyle space, indentWidth 2), linter with recommended rules.

3. In the scaffold function (lines 544-548): Replace the two writeFile calls for eslint.config.ts and .prettierrc with a single writeFile for biome.json.

4. In the README template (line 290): Change `npm run lint        # eslint` to `npm run lint        # biome`.

5. Update `platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts`: The test at line 77 checks for `eslint.config.ts` and line 79 checks for `.prettierrc` — update to check for `biome.json` instead. Lines 120-126 test scaffolded devDependencies — update expectations to check for `@biomejs/biome` instead of eslint/prettier packages.

userStories.title: Migrate all three example plugins to Biome
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the example plugins (slack, discord, e2e-test) to use Biome instead of ESLint/Prettier.
acceptanceCriteria: Each plugin (plugins/slack, plugins/discord, plugins/e2e-test) has a biome.json config file
Each plugin's eslint.config.ts is deleted
Each plugin's .prettierrc is deleted
Each plugin's package.json scripts use biome commands (lint, lint:fix, format, format:check)
Each plugin's package.json devDependencies have @biomejs/biome and no eslint/prettier packages
npm run check:plugins passes (type-check + lint + format:check for all plugins)
userStories.notes:
For each of the three plugins (plugins/slack, plugins/discord, plugins/e2e-test):

1. Create `biome.json` with plugin-appropriate settings: formatter (singleQuote, trailingCommas all, semicolons always, arrowParentheses asNeeded, lineWidth 120, bracketSameLine true, indentStyle space, indentWidth 2), linter with recommended rules, file ignores for dist/ and node_modules/.

2. Delete `eslint.config.ts` and `.prettierrc` from each plugin.

3. Update `package.json` scripts: `lint: 'biome lint src/'`, `lint:fix: 'biome lint --fix src/'`, `format:check: 'biome format src/'`, `format: 'biome format --write src/'`.

4. Update `package.json` devDependencies: remove `eslint`, `eslint-config-prettier`, `eslint-plugin-prettier`, `prettier`, `typescript-eslint`; add `@biomejs/biome` (use --save-exact, match version from root).

5. Run `cd plugins/<name> && npm install && npm run check` for each to verify.

All three plugins have identical eslint/prettier configs so the biome.json can be the same across all three. The e2e-test plugin may have slightly different typescript-eslint version — normalize to the same @biomejs/biome version.

userStories.title: Migrate docs site to Biome
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the docs site to use Biome instead of ESLint/Prettier.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/biome.json exists with appropriate settings (extends root or standalone config)
docs/eslint.config.ts is deleted
docs/.prettierrc is deleted
docs/.prettierignore is deleted
docs/package.json scripts use biome commands
docs/package.json devDependencies have @biomejs/biome and no eslint/prettier packages
docs/knip.config.ts no longer has eslint packages in ignoreDependencies
docs/tsconfig.configs.json no longer includes eslint.config.ts (if applicable)
docs/tsconfig.json no longer excludes eslint.config.ts
npm run check:docs passes (build + type-check + lint + knip + format:check)
userStories.notes:
The docs site is a standalone Next.js project in `docs/`. It has its own eslint.config.ts (128 lines, more complex than root — uses @eslint/compat, FlatCompat for react-hooks). Changes:

1. Create `docs/biome.json` — standalone config (NOT extending root, since docs is not in npm workspaces). Match formatter settings from `docs/.prettierrc` (same as root — includes tailwind plugin, which Biome replaces with useSortedClasses nursery rule). Configure linter with recommended rules, react domain, and a11y rules. Ignore `.next/`, `.content-collections/`, `next-env.d.ts`.

2. Delete `docs/eslint.config.ts`, `docs/.prettierrc`, `docs/.prettierignore`.

3. Update `docs/package.json` scripts: `lint: 'biome lint .'`, `lint:fix: 'biome lint --fix .'`, `format: 'biome format --write .'`, `format:check: 'biome format .'`.

4. Update `docs/package.json` devDependencies: remove all 14 eslint/prettier packages (`@eslint/compat`, `@eslint/eslintrc`, `@eslint/js`, `@typescript-eslint/parser`, `eslint`, `eslint-config-prettier`, `eslint-import-resolver-typescript`, `eslint-plugin-import-x`, `eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y`, `eslint-plugin-prettier`, `eslint-plugin-react`, `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`, `prettier`, `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss`, `typescript-eslint`); add `@biomejs/biome`.

5. Update `docs/knip.config.ts` (lines 14-17): remove `'@typescript-eslint/parser'` and `'eslint-plugin-react-hooks'` from `ignoreDependencies`.

6. Update `docs/tsconfig.configs.json` (line 18): remove `eslint.config.ts` from `include` array. Update `docs/tsconfig.json` (line 33): remove `eslint.config.ts` from `exclude` array.

7. Run `cd docs && npm install && npm run check` to verify.

userStories.title: Reformat entire codebase with Biome and run full verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the entire codebase formatted by Biome so there are no formatting diffs on the next commit.
acceptanceCriteria: biome format --write has been run on the entire repo
biome lint --fix has been run on the entire repo
No eslint or prettier references remain in any config file, package.json, or script
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run format:check passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
npm run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
Final story — run Biome across the entire codebase to normalize formatting and fix any remaining lint issues.

1. Run `npx biome format --write .` from repo root to reformat all files
2. Run `npx biome lint --fix .` from repo root to auto-fix any lint violations
3. Run `npx biome check .` to verify everything is clean
4. Do a grep for any remaining references: `grep -r 'eslint' --include='*.json' --include='*.ts' --include='*.md' --include='*.tsx' . | grep -v node_modules | grep -v dist | grep -v .git` — should return zero results (or only legitimate references like 'noDoubleEquals inspired from eslint' in comments that are fine)
5. Run the full verification suite: `npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check && npm run knip && npm run test && npm run test:e2e`
6. If any check fails, fix the issue. Common issues: biome may flag new lint violations that ESLint didn't catch, or the formatter may produce slightly different output that breaks snapshot tests.

This is the e2eCheckpoint story — it runs the full E2E suite to verify nothing broke.
